data.title: IB Acquisition Corp. Extends Business Combination Deadline
data.description:
IB Acquisition Corp. ( (IBAC) ) has provided an update.

IB Acquisition Corp. held a special meeting on September 22, 2025, where stockholders approved amendments to extend the deadline for completing a business combination to March 28, 2026. The amendments also outline procedures for redemption and liquidation if the company fails to complete the business combination by the new deadline. Approximately 90.96% of the company’s common stock was represented at the meeting, and stockholders approved all proposals, including the extension and trust amendments. As a result, $106.1 million will be removed from the Trust Account to pay stockholders who opted to redeem their shares, leaving approximately $15.8 million in the account.

More about IB Acquisition Corp.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: PepGen Inc. Announces Positive Phase 1 Study Results
data.description:
PepGen Inc. ( (PEPG) ) has issued an announcement.

On September 24, 2025, PepGen Inc. announced positive results from its FREEDOM-DM1 Phase 1 study, demonstrating a 53.7% mean splicing correction in patients with myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) following a single 15 mg/kg dose of PGN-EDODM1. This marks the highest splicing correction reported in DM1 patients, with all participants showing improvement. The treatment was generally well-tolerated, with mild to moderate adverse events. These findings reinforce previous data from lower dose cohorts and suggest potential for reversing molecular defects associated with DM1, which could lead to functional improvements in symptoms like myotonia and muscle weakness. PepGen plans to report further data from its ongoing FREEDOM2-DM1 study in early 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (PEPG) stock is a Sell with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on PepGen Inc. stock, see the PEPG Stock Forecast page.

The plan involves granting restricted stock units (RSUs) to eligible participants, which include employees, directors, and independent contractors, and will be settled in cash based on the fair market value of the company’s stock. The plan is designed to align the interests of participants with the company’s performance, although RSU holders will not participate in dividends.
